We recommended that the Municipal Accountant undertake the following courses of action:
Identify and explain the differences between the book and bank balances amounting to P3,562,277.09, and furnish the Office of the Auditor with the result of such identification; and

Regularly prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements for each of the bank accounts immediately after the end of each month, showing the unadjusted balances (book and bank), the reconciling items (book and bank), the adjusted balances (book and bank) and should also indicate bank account number it pertains to.  Finally, a copy of the reconciliation statement should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification.

observations.recommendation:

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly monitor the settlement of unliquidated cash advances and discontinue granting additional cash advances unless all outstanding cash advances have been liquidated.

require the Accountant to – 
Use the Certified List of taxpayers prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis for recording the RPT/SEF Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant undertake the following courses of action:
Identify and explain the differences between the book and bank balance amounting to ₱1,822,473.17 (difference in absolute value is ₱7,185,470.71), and furnish the Office of the Auditor with the result of such identification;
Identify, analyze and explain the reason why the 20% Development Fund Account has a negative balance of ₱1,410,466.91 and furnish the Office of the Auditor with the result of such identification; and
Religiously prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements for each of the bank accounts immediately after the end of each month, showing the unadjusted balances (book and bank), the reconciling items (book and bank), the adjusted balances (book and bank) and should also indicate bank account number it pertains to.  Finally, a copy of the reconciliation statement should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification.

We recommended that the Management adopt strict measures to regulate the granting of cash advances in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, and adhere to Item 6.0 of COA Circular 2012-004. Any unused or unspent cash advances should be returned or refunded immediately.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly monitor the settlement of unliquidated cash advances and discontinue granting additional cash advances unless all outstanding cash advances have been liquidated. Failure to liquidate the cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause to withhold the salary of the grantee.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze and identify the difference between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile both accounts, immediately reflect adjustments, if any, and furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of the adjustments made.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct amounts and ensure that appropriate/specific accounts are used in recording transactions. Likewise, enforce immediate settlement of the amount of P390,000.00.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the:
Municipal Accountant to prioritize the establishment of subsidiary ledgers for the respective accounts; and
Persons liable to settle their audit disallowances immediately; otherwise, based on the COA Order of Execution or COE, their salaries and any money due them shall be withheld by the Municipal Treasurer for the settlement thereof.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to take up the depreciation expense for the current year, update the related Accumulated Depreciation Accounts, as well as record adjustments in the government equity account for Prior Years’ depreciation expense not previously recorded; and
The Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to maintain the required Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC), Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC), and Property Cards, respectively.

We recommended the following to the Municipal Accountant:
To exert all efforts to reconcile and analyze the accounts to determine whether they warrant adjustment in accordance with existing COA rules and regulations;
Furnish certification and/or reasons why the books of accounts/records/financial statements/schedules and supporting documents cannot be located; and
Require proper turnover of documents/pending work from officials and employees retiring/separating from office/service to ensure completeness of records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Statement of Cash Flows accurately using the direct method so that the correct flow of cash will be presented, so that the objective of the report of aiding the Management in making a sound decision will be achieved.
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant see to it that the prescribed/specific expense accounts are used in recording and charging of transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements in accordance with IPSASs 1 and 20 to ensure that the entity is transparent about its dealings with related parties and provide users with additional information in understanding the financial statements and aid in the decision-making as well as achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the disclosure requirement of the aforementioned COA Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with all the disclosure requirements and necessary presentation in the FS in accordance with IPSAS, to achieve a fairly presented and reliable set of financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
Determine the cause of the discrepancies between the monthly remittances/refunds against the withholdings, and take the necessary action, whether to remit the difference or refund the same to the employees/suppliers, whichever is appropriate; and
Ensure that all taxes withheld during the month are remitted in their entirety on or before the date prescribed under Section 5 of RR No. 11-208 dated January 31, 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
Determine the cause of the discrepancies between the monthly remittances/refunds against the withholdings, and take the necessary action, whether to remit the difference or refund the same to the employees, whichever is appropriate; and
Ensure that all contributions withheld during the month shall be remitted in their entirety on or before the date prescribed under Section 6 (b) of RA No. 8291 and Section 3 of the IRR of RA No. 9679, respectively.

We recommended the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of the Due to PhilHealth account and upon determination, remit the balances to the concerned agency or refund the same to the concerned individuals, whichever is appropriate; and
The Management to ensure that all contributions withheld during the month shall be remitted in its entirety on or before the date prescribed under PhilHealth Circular No. 001, s. 2014 dated January 09, 2014.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Management, as the withholding agent, impose the appropriate withholding tax on the Hazard and Subsistence Allowances of the Hospital employees to avoid the incurrence of penalties, interest and surcharges for non-withholding of taxes, of which the officials and employees in charge thereof shall be personally liable.

We recommended that the Management continue its effort to collect the delinquent RPT/SET and formulate an effective strategy or policy to compel the taxpayers to pay their taxes actively and on time pursuant to the provisions of Section 249 of the Local Government Code of the Philippines.

We recommended that the Management ensures that all relevant officials, including the Local Budget Officer, Local Accountant, and Local Treasurer, receive a copy of the SEF Budget within the prescribed seven-day period from its approval to avoid delay in communication which could ultimately hinder effective monitoring and control on the utilization of the SEF funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the Quarterly and Annual Reports on SEF Utilization for the year 2024 to the concerned offices and strictly follow the submission requirements and deadlines in the future.

require all concerned officers and employees to ensure that the details in all financial reports are always complete.

We recommend that the Accounting Office comply with the provision of Section 4 (e) of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, so that all transactions would be properly documented and recorded. With this, all transactions can be readily verified and reconciled with the amount appearing in the financial statements. Thus, accuracy and reliability of the financial statements will be achieved.

We recommended that management exert more effort to ensure the collection of the delinquent RPT/SET and formulate an effective strategy or policy to compel the taxpayers to pay their taxes actively and on time pursuant to the provisions of Section 249 of the Local Government Code of the Philippines.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 271 (3)(d) of the Local Government Code and remit promptly the barangay shares in the municipal real property and community tax collections to augment the finances of the affected local government units.

We recommended that management implement all programmed projects during the year these were legislated and approved to allow early utilization by the beneficiaries thereof. Likewise, monitoring and assessment by the Local Finance Committee should also be undertaken to ensure timely completion of these projects. Further, reprogram the savings from the completed PPAs to maximize the utilization of the fund.

We recommended the accounting office to record the payment made for one (1) unit bulldozer and recognize the corresponding asset in the books in compliance with the provision of Section 112 of PD No. 1445 and to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements. We further recommended the immediate submission of the disbursement voucher and all the necessary documentary requirements for verification and audit. Otherwise the audit team will issue notice of disallowance for this disbursement.

We recommended that management require the posting of Performance Security upon signing of contract as mandated under Sections 37.2.1 and 39 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to guarantee the faithful performance by the winning bidder of its obligations under the contract, otherwise, disqualify the bidder, contractor or supplier in entering into contract with the municipality.

We recommended that the management post Notices of Award, Contracts and Notices to Proceed for all procurements undertaken through public bidding and all Requests for Quotation for alternative procurement exceeding P50,000.00 in the PhilGEPs to comply with the provisions of Sections 21 and 37 and Annex H of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA No. 9184 to promote transparency and efficiency through modernization, standardization and regulation of procurement activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned offices or municipal personnel to immediately submit the lacking documents to support the propriety, validity and legality of the transactions. Henceforth, all subsequent procurement of goods for similar purpose should be properly documented and included in the Annual Procurement Plan of the municipality.

We recommended management to:
require the Department Heads/Division Chiefs and heads of other end-user units to prepare ad submit to the BAC Secretariat their duly approved Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMPs) for their different programs, activities and projects during the year and ensure that pertinent information/data as provided under Sec. 7.3 of the IRR of RA 9184 are included therein;
require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the submitted PPMPs into an Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and to prepare the required APP for common-use supplies and equipment for submission to DBM using the standard form as set forth by the regulation; and
require the concerned Department Heads/Division Chiefs and heads of other end-user units to update their PPMPs every six (6) months or whenever necessary and for the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the updated PPMPs in the original APP.

We recommended the Management to withhold taxes in all of its procurements of goods and services in compliance with BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 to ensure that the government has it share from the income.
We recommended that management stop the paying of salaries for services yet to be fully rendered as prescribed by law and conform with the provision of Section 88 of P.D. 1445.
We recommended that the management submit the proper documentary requirements for the abovementioned BAC Honoraria otherwise the Audit Team will issue Notice of Suspension for the said transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive discontinue the practice of authorizing the grant of cash advances to officials and employees other than the designated disbursing officer and shall limit their cash advances for travelling expenses only to safeguard government funds from possible misapplication or misappropriation.

We recommended that the management enforce immediate liquidation of cash advances which are due for liquidations especially those which are long overdue in accordance with Sections 5.7 and 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
require the Accounting Office to strictly comply with the timeliness on the recording and submission of accounts as required under existing laws and regulations; and
require the Treasury Office to hasten the transmittal of all documents and reports to the Accounting Office within the most reasonable time to ensure that the latter could comply with regulation.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accounting and Treasury Offices to immediately submit all unsubmitted Check and Cash Disbursement Vouchers, including their supporting documents to the Auditor for verification and audit, pursuant to afore-cited law and regulations. Henceforth, strictly observe the prescribed period in the submission of accounts to avoid suspensions/disallowances in audit.

We recommended Management to:
strengthen its internal control;
ensure that all financial reports and forms are properly and completely accomplished to guaranty the propriety and regularity of its transactions; and
strictly adhere with the provisions of the Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs.

We recommended that the Disbursing Officer shall stamp “PAID” all the disbursement vouchers and their corresponding supporting documents after payment and indicate the check number and date of actual payment on each “Paid” stamp to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in future claims, pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-389 dated November 3, 1992 thereby safeguarding government funds against loss.

We recommended that management cause the immediate preparation of a LDRRMFIP as basis for future disbursements of the unexpended balances of LDRRMF and ensure that the same is reviewed and updated annually in accordance with the prevailing laws, rules and regulations on the use thereof.
We recommended the management to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the  Municipal
          Treasurer prepare  the  bank  reconciliation  statements  properly
          showing the unadjusted balances (book and bank),  the  reconciling
          items (book and bank) and finally the adjusted balances (book  and
          bank). The  BRS  should  also  indicate  bank  account  number  it
          pertains to.  The result of  the  reconciliation  should  be  duly
          documented and a copy of which furnished  to  the  Office  of  the
          Auditor for evaluation.

We recommended that Management direct the:


          1. Treasury  Office-  to  determine  the  current  tax  receivable
            existing as at December 31, 2017,  this  amount  should  be  the
            balance of the RPT and SET Receivable and Deferred Income;


          2.  Accounting  Office-  to  adjust  the  accounts  based  on  the
            determined amount from Treasury Office and  submit  JEV  to  COA
            Office for evaluation; and


          3. Treasury and Accounting Offices- to execute proper recording of
            RPT and SET Receivable and Deferred Income as guided by  Section
            20, Volume I of NMGAS.

We recommended the Management thru the Inventory Committee  and
          General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer to:


          1. Prepare and maintain property and ledger cards; and


          2. Immediately conduct physical count on  assets,  prepare  RPCPPE
             and reconcile the  same  with  the  accounting  records  to  be
             submitted in COA office.

We recommended that the Municipality identify the discrepancies
          between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds through  a
          reconciliation of the affected accounts and immediately effect the
          adjustments.

We recommended  that  the  Municipality  stop  paying  RATA  in
          advance to conform to the provisions of Section  88  of  P.D.  No.
          1445 and attach DTRs  to  the  monthly  payrolls  to  support  the
          services rendered and comply with the provisions of  DBM  LBC  No.
          103.

We recommended that the Accounting Office submit and attach all
          the necessary documentary requirements to all disbursements of the
          Municipality as provided under Section 4 (6) of PD  1445  and  the
          guidelines set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-001. The issuance of
          a Notice of Suspension is in order.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  strictly  monitor
          the liquidation of cash advances  such  that  expenses  chargeable
          therefrom are booked in the period to which they properly  belong.
          The Municipality is also recommended to withhold the  salaries  of
          Officers and Employees if they could not submit their  liquidation
          reports within the prescriptive period.  In  this  way,  they  are
          forced  to  either  submit  liquidation  papers  or  refund  their
          accountabilities for unused cash advances instead  of  having  the
          moneys tied-up at their hands. Management should enforce  strictly
          the cited provisions of COA Circular 97-002  to  attain  efficient
          management and utilization of government funds and resources.


      We further recommended that management  monitor  the  liquidation  of
          these outstanding cash advances by preparing  and  submitting  the
          Monthly Progress Report on Cash Advances  to  the  Office  of  the
          Auditor.

We recommended that the local government unit  comply  strictly
          with the provisions of Section 10, Rule IV and Section 48.2,  Rule
          XVI of RA No. 9184, and explain in writing why the Management  did
          not undergo public bidding on the said transaction.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


          (1) Require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials
             to promptly submit the disbursement  vouchers,  trial  balances
             and bank reconciliation statements to enable a timely and  more
             effective review of the  Agency’s  financial  transactions  and
             evaluation of its operations; and


          (2) Require all reports already due for  submission  be  submitted
             immediately most specially the financial statements.

We recommended that management  (1)  cause  the  return  of  the
          excess amount of PEI; and (2) be cognizant of  the  conditions  in
          the grant of PEI and other forms of incentives and  emoluments  to
          ensure that guidelines issued  by  competent  authorities  of  the
          government are upheld at all times.

We recommended that management: (1) submit legal basis/justification for the payment of  the additional personnel benefits; and (2) comply with the provisions of Section  2  of  PD No. 1445, Section 12 of RA No. 6758 and COA Circular No. 2013-003.

We recommended that management:


          1. earmark  1%  of  IRA  for  Programs,  Projects  and  Activities
            pertaining to measures and structures to prevent and respond  to
            abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence affecting children  as
            enshrined in RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No.  2012-
            120 and ensure full implementation thereof; and


          2. empower and mobilize the Municipal Council for  the  Protection
            of Children to enable the latter to  effectively  discharge  its
            task as the primary agency which coordinates  with  and  assists
            the LGU for the adoption of a comprehensive plan on  delinquency
            prevention, and to oversee its proper implementation. Incidental
            thereto  is  the  task  of  preparation   and   maintenance   of
            records/documentations  as  regards  monitoring  of  status   of
            implementation of the 1% of IRA.

We  recommended  that  management  stop  charging   from   20%
          Development Fund the expenses which are not covered  by  the  said
          fund in  compliance  with  DILG-DBM  Joint  Circular  No.  2011-1,
          otherwise, these will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that management:


          1. cease from granting cash advances without  any  specific  legal
             purpose/s;


          2. require/demand the immediate settlement  of  all  overdue  cash
             advances;


          3.   ensure  that  only  permanently   appointed   officials   are
             designated as disbursing officers; and


          4. grant cash advances  only  after  previous  cash  advances  are
             liquidated.

We recommended that the  Municipality,  through  the  Municipal
          Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office, prudently  evaluate
          its compliance with the Minimum Standards on Disaster Preparedness
          of the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and
          conform accordingly to its provisions to achieve desired goals and
          objectives  of  reducing  disaster  risk  and  enhancing  disaster
          preparedness and response capabilities of the LGU.

We recommended that management ensure the  immediate  settlement
          of disallowances and suspensions.

We recommended that  management  continuously  comply  with  the
          provisions of R.A. No. 9710 including its Implementing  Rules  and
          Regulations  and  to  submit  a  GAD  Accomplishment  Report   for
          validation by the audit team.

We  recommended  that  management  continuously   comply   with
          regulations prescribing remittances of mandatory contributions.

We recommended  that  management  abide  with  laws,  rules  and
          regulations governing contract of service and job order workers in
          the government.

The Municipal Accountant committed to submit all the necessary documents for the said transactions.

We recommended that the Accounting Office strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular 2009-006 on the submission of accounts for post-audit. Further, administrative and/or criminal action may be initiated by the Local Chief Executive in case of unjustified failure to comply by subordinate officials as provided under said Circular.
The Municipal Accountant committed to expedite the recording of vouchers and submit them to COA within the allotted time frame.

We recommended that the Accounting Office submit and attach all the necessary documentary requirements in all disbursements of the municipality as provided under Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and the guidelines set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-001. We further recommended that disbursement vouchers and supporting documents shall all be properly accomplished and signed before releasing the payment. Failure to comply with the said recommendation shall result to the issuance of Notice of Suspension which shall mature into disallowance if unsettled after 90 days from receipt thereof.
Management committed that for the year 2017, funds will not be released unless all of the supporting documents are not complete and all of the signatories signed the documents.

We recommended that the accounting office prepare the required forms to support each transaction of the municipality in compliance with the provisions of the Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs.
The Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 governing the operation and management of a dumpsite/sanitary landfill.
The management disclosed that they are in the process of locating possible new dump site.

We recommended that the municipal government furnish copy of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for review and evaluation as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001. Further, administrative disciplinary action shall be imposed in case of unjustified failure of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements as provided under the said Circular.
Management committed to comply with the recommendation.

We recommended Management that all requests for procurement of medicines be supported with a certification from the requisitioning officer that the medicines fall within and conform to the PNDF Volume 1.
Management committed to comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that the Management follow the computation of withholding taxes as provided under BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 to avoid error in amount of withholding taxes recorded and remitted.
The Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer produce computer printed Daily Time Records of officials and employees every month based on Biometrics data to prove the accuracy and reliability of attendance for the month and to encourage truthful reporting of the number of days worked as well as number of days of absences to be properly accounted for in the computation of salaries and wages. We further recommended for the compliance of attaching various documentary requirements to transactions to prove accuracy, validity and legality of money claims against municipal funds.
The management states that they did not attached their computer generated DTR because the biometric machine was faulty. Hence, the management will buy a new biometric machine and comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRM Fund be submitted to the audit team in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 specifically on the reporting guidelines for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) in order to aid Management in making the proper decision on the disposition of the LDRRM Fund.
The Municipal Accountant stated that they were submitting the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRM Fund but failed to submit it to the Office of the Auditor. They committed to submit the said report in CY 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Local Finance Committee review and validate the municipal expenditures and commitments in the Trust Fund to ensure that funds earmarked for these projects and liabilities are properly safeguarded and utilized for the fulfillment of the specific purposes these were received or established to comply with the provisions of Section 4 (3) of P.D. 1445 and Section 309 (b) of R.A. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The deficiency was reduced to P10,756,422.99 as at December 31, 2016
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Liquidating Officer deposit intact all the collections daily or not later than the next banking day in compliance with the provision of Section 28 of the COA Circular No. 92-382 to avoid misusage of cash on hand.
observations.managementAction: The undeposited collections was reduced to P6,230.35 as at December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 5, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the collecting officers remit daily their collections to Liquidating Officer to avoid improper and illegal use of collections in adherence with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 2013-004 and Sections 101 and 105 of PD 1445 to achieve efficient handling of accountability and management of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The shortage was restituted by the accountable officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere with the Imprest System in the setting up, payments and replenishment of Petty Cash Fund as required by the provisions of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs to ensure compliance with the established rule.
observations.managementAction: The management withdraw petty cash when the accumulated petty expenses were already reach the threshold of withdrawing the petty cash fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the Petty Cash Fund is properly used and shall only be replenished if disbursement charged to it reach at least 75% or as the need requires. Further, the disbursing officer shall liquidate the cash advances under PCF or refund the same if not used, otherwise, this will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management withdraw petty cash when the accumulated petty expenses were already reach the threshold of withdrawing the petty cash fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management identify the source of the discrepancies between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds through a reconciliation of the affected accounts and effect immediately the adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The unreconciled balance were reduced to P3,653,606.76 as at December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 8, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office reclassify/set-up the portion of the long-term loans payable that is due for payment in CY 2016 of P81,538.46 to a current liability account as prescribed by PPSAS No. 1(80).
observations.managementAction: The loans were fully paid in 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provisions of DBM BC No. 2015-2 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in granting Collective Negotiation Agreement (CNA) incentives and submit all the necessary and essential documentary requirements and other proofs that the CNA incentives were appropriately disbursed as required in the aforementioned Circulars. Otherwise, the Office of the Auditor will issue Notice of Suspension and, if not complied within the prescribed period, Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Copy of CNA and list of members were submitted to the Auditor in 2017. However, all other required documentary requirements were not submitted.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 12, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officers maintain cashbooks for all the municipal funds and record all the transactions of the municipality therein on a daily basis in compliance with the provisions of Section 6 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Chapter II Section 7 of COA Memorandum No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: Inquiry disclosed that the accountable officer were now maintaining the necessary cashbooks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management limit disbursements on Personnel Services in consonance with Section 325 (a) of R.A. 7160 to avoid audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: In 2016, PS still exceeded the 55% PS limitation. However, the excess was resulted from the increase of salary which is part of the waived item.
